2605.05091.title: Think-Aloud Reshapes Automated Cognitive Model Discovery Beyond Behavior
2605.05091.paper_summary: Computational cognitive models discovered using large language models have so far relied solely on behavioral data. However, it is well-known that models produced from the behavioral trajectory alone are typically under-determined. In this work, we explore the use of Think Aloud traces as an additional form of data constraint during automated model discovery. When applied to the domain of risky decision-making, we find that the models discovered with think-aloud achieve significantly improved predictive performance on held-out data. Additionally, we find that the discovered models belong to different structural classes than those discovered from behavior alone for the majority of participants (69.4\%), specifically, it shifts from Explicit comparator towards Integrated utility. These results suggest that process-level language data not only improve model fit, but also systematically reshape the structure of the discovered cognitive models, enabling the identification of mechanisms that are not recoverable from behavior alone.
2605.05091.paper_summary_zh: 計算認知模型的發現依賴於大型語言模型，目前為止僅依賴行為數據。然而，眾所周知，僅從行為軌跡產生的模型通常是欠確定的。在本研究中，我們探索將思考大聲痕跡作為自動模型發現過程中的另一種數據約束形式。當應用於風險決策領域時，我們發現使用思考大聲發現的模型在保留數據上的預測性能顯著提高。此外，我們發現這些發現的模型與僅從行為中發現的模型在大多數參與者（69.4%）中屬於不同的結構類別，具體而言，它從明確的比較者轉向整合效用。這些結果表明，過程級別的語言數據不僅改善了模型擬合，還系統性地重塑了發現的認知模型的結構，使得能夠識別那些僅從行為中無法恢復的機制。
2605.05091.author: Hanbo Xie et.al.
2605.05091.authors: Hanbo Xie, Akshay K. Jagadish, Lan Pan, Robert C. Wilson
